lesson.menu_title: The Executive Mandate -- Role, Remit & Track Record
lesson.menu_desc: Open a new strategic cycle with the Pittsburgh and Houston partners: frame your role, remit and track record using the present perfect simple vs continuous -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 1 -- The Executive Mandate -- Role, Remit & Track Record
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Claudia Curimbaba | Aula 1 -- The Executive Mandate

listenings.text: Maria Claudia, welcome to the new strategic cycle. I am David Chen, Operations Director at the Pittsburgh facility. For ten years you have overseen finance and sales for the holding, and you have built a remarkable track record. Our teams have been preparing for this cycle for months, and the results have been encouraging. We have consolidated two subsidiaries, and we have been streamlining the supply chain since January. The Houston stakeholders have reviewed your numbers, and they are impressed. Your mandate is clear: lead the financial and sales operations, and keep the partners aligned. You have closed three major deals this year alone, so you speak from strength. Tomorrow we open the kickoff call. Come ready to present your remit and your milestones. We have been waiting for this moment, and we are confident in your leadership.

As I open this new cycle, I want to be clear about my mandate and my remit. For ten years I have been overseeing finance and sales across the Curimbaba holding, and I have learned the business from the inside. My remit is broad: I am accountable to the board for results, and I lead operations across every subsidiary. The numbers speak for themselves. This year we have consolidated two subsidiaries, and we have closed three strategic deals. We have been streamlining our processes for months, and costs have fallen steadily. I have been building relationships with our Houston and Pittsburgh stakeholders, and the trust has grown. The turnaround of our ceramics unit was a real milestone. I have prepared carefully for this call, and I am ready to lead with authority and precision.

extra_audio.text: First, you state your mandate and remit, for example my mandate is to lead finance and sales across the holding. Then, you report your track record with the present perfect simple, such as we have closed three deals this year. After that, you describe your ongoing work with the present perfect continuous, for example we have been streamlining operations since January. Next, you highlight a milestone, such as the turnaround of the ceramics unit. Finally, you confirm that you are accountable to the board and ready to lead the new cycle with confidence.
